title: validate_regex_in_clause uses rposition without explaining why
description: Both validate_regex_in_clause (lib.rs:249-277) and cache_patterns_in_clause (lib.rs:358-383) use `.rposition()` (rightmost position) to find the Str/StringList to validate/cache. The reason is important: for a clause like `header :regex "X[Special]" "test.*"`, the header name (first Str) must NOT be validated as a regex pattern — only the key-list (last Str/StringList) should be. This is a non-obvious semantic choice that is tested by compile_regex_does_not_validate_header_name_as_pattern but not explained at the call site. The comment above the call site says nothing about why last vs first. Add a comment: '// The key-list is always the last Str/StringList in a test clause. // Earlier strings are header/address field names, not patterns.' Without this explanation a future reader will misread rposition as arbitrary and change it to position, introducing a bug.
